metadata.summary: Prepare software for multi-locale support using ICU MessageFormat, Babel/FastAPI middleware, database-driven translations, and logical CSS properties for RTL languages.

content.title: Internationalization
content.summary: Methodology for preparing software to support multiple languages and locales without code changes. Covers string externalization via ICU MessageFormat, Babel (Python) and react-intl (TS), database-driven translations, locale-aware formatting (date/number/currency), RTL layout, and the translation management workflow. Core rule: one message = one complete sentence with ICU placeholders; never concatenate translated fragments.

why.p: String concatenation breaks grammar in most locales; naive if n == 1 plural logic fails for Slavic, Arabic, and Welsh; hardcoded MM/DD/YYYY and $ symbols are wrong outside the US. ICU MessageFormat, Babel, and the Intl.* APIs handle these edge cases correctly. Retrofitting i18n after launch costs 5-10x more than building it in.

list.item: Product shipping into a second locale or with regulatory obligation (EU, Quebec Bill 96)
Externalising hardcoded strings before translation work begins
Adding RTL support (Arabic, Hebrew, Persian) — must be designed in, not retrofitted
Creating white-label products requiring locale switching
Single-locale internal tool with no expansion plan — extraction/build overhead not justified
Marketing landing pages better handled by a translation CMS (Phrase, Lokalise, Crowdin)
Domain-specific jargon that does not translate — keep English, localise only UI chrome
Using Google Translate at runtime — fluency too low for product UI

rule.statement: Never compose translated text from multiple fragments; one message = one complete sentence with ICU placeholders.
rule.rationale: Word order differs across languages. Fragment concatenation produces grammatically invalid output in most non-English locales.

rule.statement: Use ICU MessageFormat for plurals and gender/context (select); never write if n == 1 ... else.
rule.rationale: English has 2 plural forms; Russian/Ukrainian 3; Arabic 6; Welsh 6. if/else breaks for any language beyond English.

rule.statement: Use hierarchical stable keys (checkout.button.submit), never English text as the key.
rule.rationale: When copy is edited, text-as-key breaks all translation lookups in every locale. Hierarchical keys remain stable through copy changes.

rule.statement: Use CSS logical properties (margin-inline-start, text-align: start) instead of physical; set dir="rtl" on html element.
rule.rationale: Physical properties don't flip in RTL; logical properties do automatically. Flipping text-align: right instead of using start is a common agent mistake.

section.name: Best Practices
section.p: One message = one complete sentence; never compose from fragments
Use ICU MessageFormat for plurals, gender, select
Locale priority: explicit user setting > account default > Accept-Language > geo-IP > app default
Keep one source-of-truth catalogue (English) in git
Run pseudo-loc in staging — surfaces hardcoded strings, truncation, RTL bugs
Format dates/numbers/currencies via Intl.* (JS) / babel.* (Python) — never strftime

template.purpose: Babel extraction config for Python + Jinja2
